human_verification.notes: Reviewed parts and procurement boundaries, home/field service artifacts, inventory and vendor handling, DME/HME prerequisites, authorization/referral/eligibility handling, PHI controls, clinical and payer escalation rules, recall and hazardous-material handling, research-source authority mapping, and scenario-to-ability coverage.

review_notes: All required domain research scenario families are mapped to explicit scenario IDs.
All shared, service-support-and-resolution, and healthcare-and-care-delivery abilities are validated and covered.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for this validated specification-based package.
No unreviewed regressions are present in this package build.

specialist_owned_evidence: Authority boundaries are explicit for approved-vendor rules, substitute parts, warranty remedies, spend approval, legal interpretation, technician execution, licensed trade signoff, safety release, clinical judgment, payer interpretation, authorization approval, coding, claims, and PHI disclosure.

Research sources cover public healthcare regulator guidance, public payer/provider operating guidance, recognized healthcare workflow guidance, environmental and occupational safety guidance, consumer product safety guidance, and home-services consumer guidance.

Scenario map covers normal parts support, missing prerequisites, PHI handling, clinical/payer escalation, policy workaround refusal, recall and safety holds, incomplete artifacts, repeat vendor failure, communication handoff, adjacent boundary rejection, low-confidence routing, policy conflict, and tenant terminology.
Commercialization and deployment artifacts define specification-based operation and separate retained-implementation promotion criteria.
notes: The pack is safe for specification-based runtime and does not claim trusted built authority.
Promotion to retained implementation is deferred to a separate explicit build, evidence, and approval event.
